skill.name: infrastructure-reporting
skill.description: Generate network infrastructure reports for health, performance, security posture, inventory, capacity planning, and multi-site comparison.
skill.summary: Create structured infrastructure reports from network health, device, client, security, and capacity data.
skill.author: DataKnifeAI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution and weak cryptography patterns, but review found only Markdown tool-name formatting and ordinary reporting text. No executable scripts, shell commands, cryptographic code, network exfiltration, filesystem access, or prompt injection were found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False positive: Markdown tool references flagged as command execution
low_findings.description: The flagged lines use backticks to format network tool names and workflow references in Markdown. They do not contain Ruby code, shell commands, scripts, command arguments, or user-controlled execution paths.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is Markdown documentation that names tools such as get_site_health. I found no executable code or shell syntax in the skill file.

low_findings.title: False positive: reporting prose flagged as weak cryptography
low_findings.description: The flagged lines contain the skill description and infrastructure planning text. They do not reference cryptographic algorithms, hash functions, encryption routines, or credential handling.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed text is ordinary reporting and upgrade-planning language. I found no weak algorithm names or cryptographic implementation.

content.value_statement: Network teams need clear reports from scattered health, device, client, and security data. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to produce structured infrastructure reporting.
actual_capabilities: Guides creation of weekly network health reports with site health and device inventory data.
Supports monthly performance reports using device and client metrics.
Structures security and compliance audits around firewall zones, access controls, and system information.
Helps produce device inventory and capacity planning recommendations.
Supports multi-site comparison reports and executive summaries.
limitations: It depends on connected network tools and the data those tools return.
It does not include executable scripts, dashboards, or report templates as separate files.
It cannot verify live network state without access to the required tool results.
Forecasts and cost estimates require current usage data and local planning assumptions.

use_cases.title: Weekly Health Review
use_cases.description: Create a concise health report covering site status, device inventory, performance signals, risks, and recommended actions.

use_cases.title: Security Audit Package
use_cases.description: Document segmentation, access controls, system information, security posture, and compliance recommendations for review meetings.

use_cases.title: Capacity Planning Brief
use_cases.description: Summarize device usage, client trends, upgrade needs, expected timelines, and budget planning inputs.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Health Report
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a single reporting period.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a weekly network health report. Include site health, device inventory, key performance issues, risks, and prioritized next actions.

prompt_templates.title: Performance Analysis
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request focused on metrics and trends.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a monthly performance report using available device and client metrics. Highlight bottlenecks, trends, affected sites, and improvement opportunities.

prompt_templates.title: Security Compliance Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a security and compliance audit report. Review firewall zones, access controls, system information, gaps, evidence, and remediation priorities.

prompt_templates.title: Executive Capacity Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request combining technical analysis and leadership summary.
prompt_templates.prompt: Prepare an executive capacity planning report. Compare sites, summarize growth risks, recommend upgrades, estimate timelines, and list decision points.

output_examples.input: Generate a weekly health report for all sites.
output_examples.output: A report with an executive summary, site health table, device status notes, performance concerns, risk ratings, and prioritized action items.
A compliance-focused summary covering segmentation, access controls, system information, observed risks, remediation priorities, and audit-ready recommendations.
A planning brief with inventory context, usage trends, projected needs, upgrade recommendations, rough timelines, and stakeholder decision points.

best_practices: Provide the reporting period, site scope, and target audience before requesting a report.
Review tool outputs for missing sites, stale metrics, or incomplete inventory before finalizing recommendations.
Separate executive summaries from detailed technical findings for easier stakeholder review.
anti_patterns: Do not use incomplete tool results as proof of a healthy environment.
Do not present capacity forecasts without noting data age and assumptions.
Do not mix compliance evidence with unverified recommendations in the same section.

faq.question: What reports can this skill help create?
faq.answer: It supports health, performance, security, inventory, capacity planning, multi-site comparison, and executive summary reports.

faq.question: Does the skill run commands or scripts?
faq.answer: No. The skill is Markdown guidance and does not include executable scripts or shell commands.

faq.question: What data does it need?
faq.answer: It works best with site health, device inventory, device metrics, client metrics, WiFi configuration, firewall zones, and access controls.

faq.question: Can it produce compliance documentation?
faq.answer: Yes. It can structure audit documentation around security posture, access controls, segmentation, evidence, and recommendations.

faq.question: Can it compare multiple sites?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes guidance for comparing health and performance across sites when multi-site data is available.

faq.question: Is this suitable for executives?
faq.answer: Yes. It can produce executive summaries with key findings, risks, priorities, and action timelines.
